title: SS21/15 – Internal Governance

related_policy_statements.title: PS9/17 - Implementation of MiFID II: Part 2

boe_references.title: The Supervisory Statement (SS) shown on this page is effective from 28/04/2017. Past versions of this SS can be found here.
PS9/17 - Implementation of MiFID II: Part 2

paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement (SS) is relevant to banks, building societies and Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) designated investment firms. It sets out the expectations of the PRA in relation to h
The PRA expects the matters dealt with in a business firm’s continuity policy to include the following:

The two independent minds should be involved in the decision-making process on all significant decisions. Both should demonstrate the qualities and application to influence strategy, day-to-day policy

The PRA expects that CRR firms should, taking account of their size, nature and complexity, consider whether their risk control arrangements should include:

The PRA expects that where a firm is part of a group it will structure its arrangements so that a Chief Risk Officer at an appropriate level within the group will exercise functions in 2.28 taking int
The Chief Risk Officer should be accountable to a firm’s governing body.
Firms should ensure that a Chief Risk Officer’s remuneration is subject to approval by the firm's governing body, or an appropriate sub-committee.

The PRA expects that a governing body risk committee’s responsibilities will typically include:
